levels.label: Premature
levels.copy: The Fundraising Readiness Radar pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Preparing
levels.copy: Some Fundraising Readiness Radar practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Pitchable
levels.copy: Core Fundraising Readiness Radar practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Investable
levels.copy: Fundraising Readiness Radar practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Process-Ready
levels.copy: Fundraising Readiness Radar is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.narrative-and-ambition: Rewrite the pitch around customer pain, why now, wedge, and credible expansion, not product features.
recommendations.evidence-and-traction: Select the three metrics most likely to change an investor belief and improve their evidence quality.
recommendations.process-and-targeting: Build a partner-level investor scorecard and run outreach in a concentrated, trackable process.
recommendations.deal-readiness: Tie the round to milestones, update the cap table, and complete a diligence readiness review.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
